description: 'shekoi' takes a preceding general statement and narrows it to the particular case or detail the speaker means. English commonly renders it specifically or in particular. 'phisu' (EXMP) offers one illustration among possible examples; 'shekoi' identifies the part now at issue.
articulatory_notes: Raise the tongue blade for unvoiced postalveolar 'sh' and release it into unrounded front 'e'. Close the tongue dorsum against the soft palate for 'k', then release into stressed rounded mid-back 'o'. Keep final high-front 'i' in its own syllable and unround the lips cleanly. Stress 'ko'.
sound_symbolism: The discourse breath in 'sh' gives way to a firm closure at 'k' and two distinct vowel syllables. That brief tightening can be felt as the marker narrowing attention to one point.
usage_notes: A Slot 0 particle precedes 'shekoi'; the subject of the narrowed sentence follows. That sentence may repeat the broader predicate or replace it with the more exact claim the speaker intends.

examples.phi: mia lo shiro lothea. shekoi mia ha serao shiro lothea.
examples.translation: I love trees. Specifically, I love this old tree.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: 'shekoi' gives a speaker a grammatical way to replace a broad charge with the particular event or claim at issue.
